seoTitle: Stonehenge vs Stony Head — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Stonehenge and Stony Head. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Stonehenge vs Stony Head: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Stonehenge and Stony Head.
heading: Stonehenge vs Stony Head — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Stonehenge, Tasmania and Stony Head, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Stony Head has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Stonehenge (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Stonehenge or Stony Head safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Stonehenge has a flood risk score of 39/100, while Stony Head scores 26/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Stonehenge scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. Stony Head scores 52/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Stonehenge or Stony Head?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Stony Head has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.03% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
